title: Weyl spin-momentum locking in a chiral topological semimetal
type1_summary: No clearly replot-ready cleaned/tabular figure data were identifiable from the available file inspection reports.
type2_summary: Both files are Nature source-data PDFs in a paper that explicitly deposits primary and processed data; conservatively treated as raw/unprocessed or source-data supporting the ARPES and spin-resolved measurements.
classification_notes: The paper clearly contains both raw measurements and processed/theoretical results, but the two provided files are only source-data PDFs. Without internal parsing, they are conservatively classified as Type 2. If the PDFs contain extracted tables or figure-specific panels, that would require deeper inspection to reclassify.

paper_analysis.summary: This paper reports spin- and angle-resolved photoemission evidence that the chiral topological semimetal PtGa hosts Weyl-type spin-momentum locking in both the bulk multifold fermions and the associated Fermi-arc surface states. Soft X-ray ARPES directly images the bulk multifold crossings at Γ and R, while spin-resolved VUV-ARPES shows that the Fermi arcs near Γ have spins orthogonal to the arc contour, consistent with parallel spin-momentum locking inherited from the bulk states.
measurement_types: crystal growth / characterization (Laue backscattering, EDX, single-crystal X-ray diffraction)

figures.description: Schematic comparison of the three linear spin-momentum locking types: Rashba (orthogonal), Dresselhaus (mixed), and Weyl (parallel).
Calculated three-dimensional spin texture of the two spherical Fermi surfaces formed by the S=3/2 Rarita-Schwinger-Weyl multifold fermion in PtGa near Γ.
Conceptual illustration linking bulk multifold-fermion spin texture to the spin texture of topological Fermi-arc surface states.
Soft X-ray ARPES bulk band dispersion along X-R-X showing the sixfold crossing at R.
Ab initio calculation corresponding to the bulk band dispersion near R.
Soft X-ray ARPES bulk band dispersion along M-Γ-M showing the fourfold Rarita-Schwinger-Weyl fermion near Γ.
Ab initio calculation corresponding to the bulk band dispersion near Γ.
VUV-ARPES Fermi surface map of the (001) surface showing Fermi arcs near Γ and around the projected R pocket.
Calculated surface-state/Fermi-arc spectrum for PtGa on the (100) surface.
Photon-energy-dependent VUV-ARPES map showing no out-of-plane dispersion of the Fermi arcs.
Band dispersion along Γ-R showing four Fermi arcs merging into the Γ-point multifold projection.
Spin-resolved measurements of the two Fermi arcs close to Γ, showing in-plane spin polarization orthogonal to the Fermi contour.
Calculated spin texture of the Fermi arcs around Γ, matching the experiment.
Spin-resolved measurements of the Fermi arcs between Γ and R, where the spin texture is predominantly tangential to the contour.
Calculated Fermi-arc spin texture between Γ and R.
Quantified in-plane spin-polarization asymmetries along several momentum cuts for the Fermi arcs.
Spin cuts near Γ measured with different light polarizations (s, p, circular helicities) to test matrix-element effects.
Extracted in-plane and out-of-plane spin-polarization directions for the two surface states under different light polarizations.
VUV-ARPES band dispersion along R-Y at hv = 74 eV showing the lower branch of the multifold fermion at R.
Calculated spin texture around R for the band corresponding to the measured branch in Fig. 5a.
Spin polarization versus binding energy at several momenta around R for different light polarizations.
Extracted spin-polarization orientation and magnitude for the bulk band near R, compared with the Weyl-SML expectation.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: The raw measurements are ARPES spectra acquired on PtGa single crystals: soft X-ray ARPES band dispersions, VUV-ARPES Fermi-surface maps, photon-energy-dependent kz scans, spin-resolved MDCs, and spin-resolved EDCs. Measurements were performed at multiple synchrotron beamlines/endstations using SPECS, Scienta R4000, VLEED, Mott, and Ferrum-VLEED spin detectors, with different light polarizations and photon energies.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The paper states: 'The primary and processed data generated in this study have been deposited in the Open Research Data Repository of the Max Planck Society under accession code 10.17617/3.GIT9JN [https://doi.org/10.17617/3.GIT9JN].' It also says custom scripts are available in the data repository.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw data are the ARPES measurements generated in the study, but the paper does not specify file formats. The measurements come from multiple instruments/endstations: SX-ARPES at ADRESS, VUV-ARPES at Diamond I05, spin-resolved VUV-ARPES at APE-LE, COPHEE, and Bloch-B. The repository is said to contain both primary and processed data.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include extracted spin-polarization values, fitted spin directions, and figure-ready spectra/maps used in Figs. 2-5. The paper describes numerical correction of instrumental asymmetry, gain adjustment between detectors, vectorial fitting of spin expectation values, and background-referenced extraction for EDC analysis.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Yes. The paper implies figure-specific data are provided because the repository contains primary and processed data, and the analysis uses figure-specific extracted quantities for the main figures. However, the paper does not explicitly enumerate per-figure source-data files.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: Yes. The paper explicitly mentions 'custom scripts available in the data repository' and also references the vectorial fitting routine from Ref. 45.
dataset_characterization.notes: The dataset is a mixed experimental/theoretical package. Experimental ARPES data are the primary measurements; processed data are derived spin polarizations, fitted spin vectors, and figure-ready cuts/maps. Theoretical data include DFT and WannierTools surface-state calculations. The paper does not mention notebooks, but does mention custom scripts and external software (FPLO, WannierTools, MATools). The repository likely contains both raw and processed data, but the exact internal file structure is not described in the text.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The paper says 'The primary and processed data generated in this study have been deposited...' and describes multiple direct ARPES measurements as the basis of the figures.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: The paper explicitly mentions processed data, extracted spin-polarization values, fitted spin directions, and custom scripts in the repository.
classification_prior.data_availability_section_relevant: ‘The primary and processed data generated in this study have been deposited in the Open Research Data Repository of the Max Planck Society under accession code 10.17617/3.GIT9JN.’; ‘custom scripts available in the data repository’; ‘For the calculation of the spin polarization, the instrumental asymmetry was corrected...’; ‘To quantify the orientation ... we used a 3D vectorial fit...’
